content: Find a ETF ticker that I can make some profit using polymarket forecast tool

args.thought: User wants an ETF recommendation based on Polymarket forecast for profit potential. I need to: 1) Identify ETFs with clear Polymarket catalyst exposure, 2) Run polymarket_forecast on candidates, 3) Compare expected returns vs confidence intervals to find asymmetric opportunities. From prior analysis, USO showed asymmetric spike potential. Let me systematically check several ETFs with different catalyst exposures.

Need to identify ETFs with strong Polymarket signal coverage. Best candidates: TLT (rate cuts), GLD (recession/geopolitical), USO (oil/OPEC), SPY (broad market). Will run polymarket_forecast on each to compare expected returns and confidence grades.

args.query: current prices for TLT GLD USO SPY ETFs

results.title: The 1-Minute Market Report, March 29, 2026 (NYSEARCA:SPY) | Seeking Alpha
highlights:
The 1-Minute Market Report, March 29, 2026 (NYSEARCA:SPY) | Seeking Alpha

Erik Conley

13.44K Followers

5 Share

Play( 7min)

- Comments(3)

## Summary - The S&P 500 is down 7.4% for March, with the decline accelerating and large caps, especially the Mag 7, driving losses. - Investors are rotating out of large-cap growth and AI hyperscalers. - Energy, materials, utilities, and consumer staples outperformed. - Crosscurrents show persistent de-risking, with foreign equities, yield, and defensive names attracting capital as U.S. large caps remain under pressure. SlavkoSereda/iStock via Getty Images Now that the S&P 500 has closed firmly below the -5% line, the next level of support is the -10% line, which is roughly 6300. What began as an orderly pullback has turned into a fear-driven slide. Today we’ll go through the charts This article was written by

Erik Conley

13.44K Followers I spent 30 years in the institutional trenches as a trader, analyst, and portfolio manager, eventually running the equity trading desk at Northern Trust in Chicago. Those decades shaped my approach: stay disciplined, trust the data, and keep emotion out of the way. Since 2009, when I began publishing my stock selections, my portfolio has delivered solid long term results—compounding in the mid teens annually through 2025. Today I’m a private investor and investing   ...    zeninvestor.org. . Analyst’s Disclosure: I/we have a beneficial long position in the shares of NVDA, AVGO, GOOGL either through stock ownership, options, or other derivatives. I wrote this article myself, and it expresses my own opinions. I am not receiving compensation for it (other than from Seeking Alpha). I have no business relationship with any company whose stock is mentioned in this article. Seeking Alpha's Disclosure: Past performance is no guarantee of future results. No recommendation or advice is being given as to whether any investment is suitable for a particular investor. Any views or opinions expressed above may not reflect those of Seeking Alpha as a whole. args.thought: All 4 ETF forecasts show identical -0.69% returns - the model is using generic macro signals without asset-specific differentiation. This isn't useful for finding asymmetric opportunities. Need to: 1) Check ETFs with more specific Polymarket catalyst exposure (defense, energy, volatility), 2) Consider current market momentum from search results (GLD +3.51%, USO +5.92%, defensive rotation), 3) Look for ETFs where Polymarket has asset-specific markets (e.g., oil price targets, gold targets, defense spending).

The generic macro signals produce identical forecasts across all ETFs. To find asymmetric opportunities, I need ETFs with specific Polymarket market exposure: 1) Defense ETFs (geopolitical conflict markets), 2) Energy ETFs (oil price specific markets), 3) Volatility ETFs (VIX-related markets), 4) Crypto ETFs (Bitcoin/ETH specific markets). Let me search for Polymarket markets on these specific topics and match them to ETF tickers.

args.thought: The generic macro forecasts produced identical -0.69% returns across all ETFs - the model wasn't using asset-specific Polymarket markets. However, my Polymarket searches revealed meaningful asset-specific signals: 1) Oil: 80.8% chance of hitting $100 by end of March (very high conviction), 2) Bitcoin: 55.1% chance of dipping to $65K in March (moderate bearish signal), 3) Gold: mostly extreme low-probability events. The oil signal is the strongest asymmetric opportunity - 80.8% probability of $100+ oil by March 31 is a clear directional bet. USO is at $124.20, so if oil hits $100, USO should benefit. Need to get Bitcoin ETF tickers and run focused forecasts on oil/Bitcoin ETFs with these specific signals.
